name: Making Your Own Devonshire or Clotted Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. In winter, let the fresh, unpasteurized cream stand for 12 hours (in summer, about 6 hours) in a heat-proof dish.
2. Put the cream on to heat using the lowest possible setting. Do not boil the cream, as this will coagulate the albumen and ruin the mixture.
3. When small rings or undulations form on the surface of the cream, it is sufficiently scalded. Remove the cream immediately from the heat.
4. Store the scalded cream in a cold place for at least 12 hours.
5. Skim off the thick, clotted layer and serve very cold as a garnish for berries or spread on scones topped with jam.
